title: Elephantella groenlandica
intro: Elephantella groenlandica, also known as Greenlandic or Iceland moss, is a type of lichen that belongs to the family Cladoniaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Elephantella groenlandica include polysaccharides, vitamins, and minerals, as well as compounds like glycosaminoglycans and flavonoids. In traditional herbal medicine, Elephantella groenlandica is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to make cough syrups, lozenges, and other respiratory remedies.
uses: Elephantella groenlandica is a plant species native to Greenland and the Arctic tundra.
